## Session — 2026-04-14

Goal: Complete PSD-Home Task H1 — API + types for Home only (@docs/PSD-Home.md §2, §7). Implement TMDB access in @src/api/movies.ts and response/request types in @src/api/types.ts for: trending (week), top rated, genre list, and discover (optional with_genres + page). No Home UI, hooks, or navigation in this task.

---

## Scope

**movies.ts** — Add clear exported functions using the shared Axios client only (no new axios imports):

- GET /trending/movie/week — `page`
- GET /movie/top_rated — `page`
- GET /genre/movie/list
- GET /discover/movie — `page`; optional `with_genres` when filtering by genre; when `with_genres` is omitted, no genre filter (future “All” chip).

**types.ts** — Strict DTOs for list responses, movie list items, genres, pagination (`total_pages`, `total_results`, etc.) where TMDB returns them. No `any`.

**Pagination** — List helpers accept `page`; types match paginated payloads.

**Errors** — Follow @src/api/client.ts interceptors; do not duplicate global error normalization in movies.ts.

---

## Out of scope

useHome, HomeScreen, navigation / See All, skeletons, genre chip UI, infinite scroll / “within 3 items” UX, tab bar, image components, new dependencies.

---

## Verify

- Project typechecks.
- No `import axios` outside `src/api/`.
- New exports are ready for H2 (useHome).

---

## Deliverable

- Files changed (list).
- Short mapping: each movies.ts export → HTTP path + main query params.

_**Agent (composer-2)**_


Here’s what was done for **H1**.

### Files changed
- `src/api/types.ts` — paginated list DTOs + discover query type  
- `src/api/movies.ts` — `getDiscoverMovies` export  

### What changed
1. **`TmdbPagedMoviesResponse`** — added **`total_results`** so list responses match TMDB (trending, top rated, discover, search).  
2. **`DiscoverMoviesParams`** — `extends PaginationParams` with optional **`with_genres?: number`** (omit for “All” / no genre filter).  
3. **`PageMeta`** — added **`total_results`** so it lines up with paginated payloads.  
4. **`getDiscoverMovies`** — `GET /discover/movie` with **`page`** (default 1) and **`with_genres`** only when defined (no duplicate error handling; still uses `client` only).

Existing helpers already matched the spec: **`getTrendingMoviesWeek`**, **`getTopRatedMovies`**, **`getMovieGenres`**, plus **`getHomeData`** (parallel bundle for H2).

### Verify
- `npx tsc --noEmit` — **passes**  
- `npm run lint` — **passes**  
- `import axios` — **only** in `src/api/client.ts`  

---

### Export → HTTP + params

| Export | Method / path | Main query params |
|--------|----------------|-------------------|
| `getTrendingMoviesWeek` | `GET /trending/movie/week` | `page` (default `1`) |
| `getTopRatedMovies` | `GET /movie/top_rated` | `page` (default `1`) |
| `getMovieGenres` | `GET /genre/movie/list` | _(none)_ |
| `getDiscoverMovies` | `GET /discover/movie` | `page` (default `1`); optional `with_genres` (genre id; omitted = no filter) |
| `getHomeData` *(bundle)* | Uses `getTrendingMoviesWeek` + `getTopRatedMovies` + `getMovieGenres` | `page: 1` on both list calls |

*(Unchanged from before H1 but still part of the module: `getConfigurationSmoke`, `searchMovies`, `getMovieDetail`.)*

H2 can use **`TmdbPagedMoviesResponse.total_pages`** / **`total_results`** for **`hasMore`**, and **`getDiscoverMovies`** for the genre row with or without **`with_genres`**.

_**User**_

## Session — 2026-04-14

Goal: Update `rules/components.mdc` with (1) **folder layout** matching project-spec §5 and (2) **reusability** guidance. Keep existing sections (presentational role, FlatList, styling, data) — **minimal diff**, no full rewrite.

---

## Context (attach)

@rules/components.mdc
@rules/movielist-project.mdc

---

## Requirements

### Folder layout

Add a **“Folder layout”** section:

- **`src/components/common/`** — components used in **2+ screens** or clearly shared (e.g. `ContentCard`, badges, shared list rows).
- **`src/components/<screen-name>/`** — components **only** used by one screen/feature; name the folder after that area (`home`, `detail`, `search`, …).
- **`src/screens/*.tsx`** — compose hooks + components; avoid giant inline JSX; split into `components/` when a block grows or could be reused.

### Reusability

Add a **“Reusability”** section:

- **Extract** to `common/` when the same UI pattern appears twice or is likely to (cards, chips, section headers, rating badge).
- **Keep** in `screen-name/` when behaviour/props are **specific** and unlikely to repeat — do not force abstraction prematurely.
- **Props in, UI out** — presentational; no TMDB fetching inside dumb components (parents/hooks pass data).
- Prefer **composition** (small building blocks) over one mega-component file.

### Constraints

- Do not duplicate stack/env/hooks rules from `movielist-project.mdc`.
- Keep frontmatter unchanged unless `globs` must stay `src/components/**/*`.
- Short imperative bullets only.

---

## Deliverable

What you added (summary) + the new section text.

### Summary

In **`.cursor/rules/components.mdc`**, two sections were added **after Role** and **before Lists**: **Folder layout** (where `common/`, `<screen-name>/`, and screen composition fit) and **Reusability** (when to extract vs keep local, presentational boundary, composition). **Frontmatter**, **Role**, **Lists**, **Styling**, **Portrait content cards**, and **Data** are unchanged aside from this insertion.

### New section text

```markdown
## Folder layout

- **`src/components/common/`** — components used in **2+ screens** or clearly shared (e.g. `ContentCard`, badges, shared list rows).
- **`src/components/<screen-name>/`** — components **only** for one screen or feature; name the folder after that area (`home`, `detail`, `search`, …).
- **`src/screens/*.tsx`** — compose hooks + components; avoid giant inline JSX; split into **`components/`** when a block grows or could be reused.

## Reusability

- **Extract** to **`common/`** when the same UI pattern appears twice or is likely to (cards, chips, section headers, rating badge).
- **Keep** in **`<screen-name>/`** when behaviour or props are **specific** and unlikely to repeat — do not force abstraction prematurely.
- **Props in, UI out** — presentational; **no TMDB fetching** inside dumb components (parents or hooks pass data).
- Prefer **composition** (small building blocks) over one mega-component file.
```

---

_**User**_

## Session — 2026-04-14

Goal: PSD-Home Task H2 — implement `@src/hooks/useHome.ts` data layer per @docs/PSD-Home.md §2.2 and §7 (H2). Load genre list, derive hero from trending, run three rows (Trending, Top Rated, Genre/discover) with **independent** `page`, append-on-load-more state, and `hasMore`. Genre chip selection is **local React state** in the hook (or returned setters) and only changes the **third row**’s genre + reset pagination for that row. **No full Home UI** (header, hero layout, cards, gradients) — hook + minimal wiring to prove data only if needed.

---

## Context (attach)

@docs/PSD-Home.md
@docs/PSD-Architecture.md
@docs/API-Reference.md
@src/api/movies.ts
@src/api/types.ts
@src/hooks/useHome.ts
@rules/hooks.mdc
@rules/movielist-project.mdc
@rules/code-quality.mdc

---

## Requirements

1. **`useHome`** calls **only** `@src/api/movies.ts` (no axios). Align with the **UseQueryResult** contract from your project (`data`, `loading`, `error`, `refetch`) **or** one **documented** structured return (e.g. `{ hero, rows, genres, ... }`) that still exposes loading/error/refetch behaviour — note the choice in a short comment at the top of the hook file.

2. **Genre list** — Fetch/cache `@src/api/movies.ts` genre list; map chip labels (All, Action, Drama, Comedy, Sci-Fi, Horror, Documentary) to TMDB genre IDs per PSD; handle missing names safely.

3. **Hero** — From **first** trending result when available (or PSD rule you document); loading until first trending payload resolves.

4. **Three rows** — Independent state each: `data`, `loading`, `error`, `page`, `hasMore`, append when loading more. Rows: Trending, Top Rated, Genre row (discover with optional `with_genres`).

5. **Chips** — Selected chip is **local state**; changing chip updates **only** row 3’s genre + resets page / refetches discover for that row.

6. **No navigation** inside the hook (`navigation.navigate`).

7. **Strict TypeScript**, no `any`.

---

## Out of scope

Home **UI** composition (H4), See All route/screen (H3), scroll “within 3 items” load-more (H5), skeletons/empty UI (H6), tab bar/blur, new npm packages.

---

## Verify

- iOS + Android: open app; if you add a **minimal** debug readout on Home, hero + three rows show **real TMDB-driven** state (or log). No axios outside `src/api/`.

---

## Deliverable

- Files changed; brief note on hook return shape and how each row paginates independently.

**Reviewing genre increment**

I discovered that genreDiscoverGenRef is incremented twice on bootstrap: once in the initial block and again in the chip effect, causing unexpected behavior. I found that genreDiscoverGenRef is incremented at the start of chip effects, causing a race condition with bootstrap initialization. 

**Fixing genre increment**

I fixed the race condition by ensuring `discoverGenAtBootstrapStart` captures the old value before incrementing. I confirmed the fix prevents race conditions by ensuring bootstrap captures the correct generation reference. I identified a double increment bug where chip effects incorrectly incremented genreDiscoverGenRef at bootstrap start, causing race conditions. I confirmed the bootstrap increment behavior and validated that loadMore correctly uses the pre-incremented genreDiscoverGenRef value. 

**Updating imports**

I merged type and value imports from types.ts in useHome. 

**Handling refetch race**

I found a race condition where chip fetches become stale during refetch because chip's discoverGen doesn't match genreDiscoverGenRef.current. I'm ensuring chip completion skips when bootstrap runs by comparing discoverGen with ref, and updating HomeScreen to use immutable genres copy. I'm setting `error` to `genresError` for top-level error display and keeping row errors separate for simplicity. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2)**_

Here is what was implemented for **H2**.

### Files changed
- `src/api/types.ts` — `HomeChipKey`, `HOME_CHIP_DEFINITIONS`, `HomeChipResolved`, `HomeFeedRowState`, `createInitialHomeFeedRowState`, `UseHomeResult`
- `src/hooks/useHome.ts` — full data hook (genres, hero, 3 rows, chips, load-more, refetch, race guards)
- `src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx` — minimal debug UI (hero line, chip strip, row counts/pages/`hasMore`, three “More …” buttons, errors)

### Hook return shape (documented in `useHome.ts`)
The hook returns **`UseHomeResult`**, not `UseQueryResult<HomeData>`:
- **Top-level:** `loading` (initial bootstrap: genres + page 1 for all three rows), `error` (genre list failure only), `refetch` (re-runs that bootstrap; chip choice preserved via ref).
- **Hero:** `hero` = `trending.items[0] ?? null`; `heroLoading` = first trending page not settled yet (`page === 0` and still loading).
- **Chips:** `chips` (PSD order + resolved TMDB ids, `null` when the name is missing from `/genre/movie/list`), `selectedChipKey`, `setSelectedChipKey`.
- **Rows:** `trending`, `topRated`, `genre` — each `HomeFeedRowState`: `items`, `loading`, `loadingMore`, `error`, `page`, `hasMore`.
- **Append:** `loadMoreTrending`, `loadMoreTopRated`, `loadMoreGenre` (each uses its own `page` / `hasMore`; **discover** uses current chip + `genresRef` for `with_genres`).
- **Extra:** `genres` (copy of list), `genresError` (also shown on Home when present).

### Independent pagination
Each row keeps its **own** React state. **Load-more** only bumps that row’s `page`, appends `results`, and sets `hasMore` from `page < total_pages`. **Chip changes** only bump `genreDiscoverGenRef`, reset **row 3**, and refetch discover; **`dataGenerationRef` + `genreDiscoverGenRef`** avoid stale writes when bootstrap, chip refetch, and load-more overlap.

### Chip → TMDB mapping
`HOME_CHIP_DEFINITIONS` maps **Sci-Fi** → TMDB name **`Science Fiction`**; ids come from **`getMovieGenres`** with case-insensitive name match. Missing names yield `genreId: null` on `chips`; discover for that chip uses `discoverParamsForChip` and surfaces an error if the id cannot be resolved.

### Verify
- `npx tsc --noEmit`, `npm run lint`, `npm test` — all pass.  
- **Simulators:** not run here; with a valid `.env` TMDB token, Home should show the debug hero, chips, and row stats after load.

### Tests
No new hook tests (would need `renderHook` + mocked `movies.ts`). Reasonable follow-up: extract pure chip-resolution helpers and unit-test them, or add an integration test with MSW-style mocks.

_**User**_

## Session — 2026-04-14

Goal: PSD-Home Task H3 — add **See All** flow: extend navigation types and stack, implement a minimal `SeeAllScreen` that lists movies from the same TMDB modes as Home rows (`trending` | `top_rated` | `discover` + optional `genreId`). Functional list only — polish matches H4/H5 later.

---

## Context (attach)

@docs/PSD-Home.md
@docs/PSD-Architecture.md
@docs/API-Reference.md
@src/navigation/types.ts
@src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx
@src/api/movies.ts
@src/api/types.ts
@rules/navigation.mdc
@rules/movielist-project.mdc
@rules/hooks.mdc
@rules/code-quality.mdc

---

## Requirements

1. **Types** — Extend the **Home stack** param list (names aligned with your repo) with a route such as **`SeeAll`**:  
   `{ title: string; mode: 'trending' | 'top_rated' | 'discover'; genreId?: number }`  
   (Adjust if your stack uses different route names — keep fully typed.)

2. **Navigator** — Register **`SeeAllScreen`** on the same stack as Home main + Detail; **`navigation.navigate('SeeAll', params)`** must be type-safe.

3. **`SeeAllScreen`** — Under `@src/screens/` (or split presentational pieces under `@src/components/home/` if you prefer). Screen uses a **hook or inline fetch pattern consistent with your project** — if hooks-only for data, add a small **`useSeeAll`** in `@src/hooks/` that calls **`movies.ts`** only (no axios in screen). Minimal UI: title from params, **`FlatList`** of cards or text rows, pull or button **retry** optional.

4. **Data** — Map `mode` (+ optional `genreId`) to existing **`movies.ts`** functions from H1 (trending / top_rated / discover). Support **pagination** at least for **page 1** in this task; append next pages optional (H5 can extend).

5. **Strict TypeScript**, no `any`. No new npm dependencies.

---

## Out of scope

Full Home UI (H4), hero, genre strip styling, “within 3 items” infinite scroll (H5), skeletons (H6), glass tab bar, wiring every Home row “See All” tap with perfect design — wire **one** working path from Home if trivial, otherwise screen can be opened via dev navigation for this task.

---

## Verify

- From Home stack, navigate to See All with params → list loads; back returns to Home. Typecheck passes.

---

## Deliverable

Files changed + short note: route name, param shape, which `movies.ts` functions each mode uses.
